reason: Response A follows the conventional letter template Haiku defaults to — placeholder fields ([Friend's name], [Your name]), enthusiastic exclamation points, and standard congratulatory beats ("doesn't surprise me," "you deserved it," "your team is lucky to have you"). Response B is more crafted and understated, with measured, almost literary observations ("Not because everything will be easy, but because you are the kind of person who figures things out"), which reads like a stronger model imitating warmth with extra polish. The straightforward, formula-following warmth of A is more characteristic of genuine Haiku.
